channel.title: Sergey Lyapustin — AI Architect
channel.description: Notes on AI, engineering, and building things that matter. AI agent architecture, conversation intelligence, and LLM integration.

item.title: When Building Is the Easy Part: The New Role of the Engineer
item.dc:creator: Sergey Lyapustin
item.description: AI collapsed the cost of building software. When anyone can ship a product in a weekend, what does that mean for engineers? The value is shifting from implementation to strategy, domain expertise, and distribution.

item.title: Supabase: The Database AI Agents Deserve
item.dc:creator: Sergey Lyapustin
item.description: Why Supabase is quietly becoming the best database platform for AI agent applications — real-time subscriptions, pgvector, Row Level Security, and Edge Functions in one unified stack.

item.title: Building AI Agents That Actually Work
item.dc:creator: Sergey Lyapustin
item.description: Lessons learned from shipping production AI agents — what the tutorials don't tell you about reliability, cost, and user trust.

item.title: RAG Is Not Enough: When to Go Beyond Retrieval
item.dc:creator: Sergey Lyapustin
item.description: RAG got us far, but some problems need more. Exploring fine-tuning, tool use, and hybrid approaches for complex AI systems — and a practical layered architecture that uses the right technique for each query.

item.title: The Language-Agnostic Engineer
item.dc:creator: Sergey Lyapustin
item.description: Why I stopped identifying with a single programming language and started focusing on outcomes. A case for tools over tribes — and how it changed the way I architect systems.
